name: Miniature Bagels
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water; let stand until bubbly, about 10 minutes.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine 2 cups of flour, sugar, and salt. Gradually beat in the yeast mixture and 1 cup of water on low speed.
3. Beat on medium speed for 5 minutes. Stir in the remaining flour to form a soft but workable dough.
4. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface; knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
5. Place the dough in a lightly greased bowl; turn the greased side up. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 30 minutes (the dough will not double).
6. Punch down the dough; turn it onto a lightly floured surface. Knead slightly.
7. Divide the dough into two halves. Roll one half of the dough into a 12-inch square. Cut into 24 strips, 6x1 inch.
8. Shape each strip into a ring by pinching the ends together. Place the rings 1 inch apart on ungreased baking sheets. Repeat with the remaining dough.
9. Cover and let rise in a warm place for another 30 minutes.
10. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
11. In a Dutch oven, bring 4 inches of water to a boil.
12. Reduce heat; simmer the bagel rings, a few at a time, uncovered, for 7 minutes, turning once.
13. Remove with a slotted spoon and let stand on kitchen towels for 5 minutes.
14. Place the bagels on ungreased baking sheets.
15. Bake until firm, about 10 minutes.
16. In a small bowl, mix egg white and 1 tablespoon water.
17. Remove the bagels from the oven; brush with the egg white mixture.
18. Sprinkle with poppy seeds, sesame seeds, or onion flakes.
19. Return to the oven and bake until brown, about 20 minutes. Remove and let cool on a wire rack.
(68 calories per serving) 1 serving = 1 Bread exchange
